lesson.menu_desc: Shopping for gifts in a Paris gift shop -- choosing with 'one / ones' + Which one...? -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Buying Gifts & Making Choices
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gabriela Pires | Aula 17 -- Buying Gifts & Making Choices | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hello, and welcome to our gift shop! We have many beautiful gifts for your family and friends. On this shelf, you can see the postcards. The big ones are two euros, and the small ones are only one euro. Over there, we have keychains in many colors. The blue one is very popular with visitors. Next to them, there is jewelry; the silver ones are a little more expensive, but they are perfect for a present. We also have fridge magnets and leather wallets. If you need wrapping paper for a gift, just ask me. Take your time, choose your favorites, and if you are not sure, I can help you. Which one would you like to see first?

Today I went to a gift shop in Paris to buy presents for my family. First, I looked at the postcards. There were big ones and small ones, and I took three small ones for my friends. Then I saw the keychains. The clerk asked me, which one would you like? I said, I'll take the blue one, please. For my mom, I wanted something special, so I chose a beautiful piece of jewelry. I also bought a fridge magnet for my dad and a leather wallet for my brother. The clerk wrapped everything in nice wrapping paper. It was so much fun to choose the perfect gift for each person, and I asked for everything in English!

extra_audio.text: First, you walk into the gift shop and you look at the gifts. Next, you find the postcards and you choose the small ones. After that, the clerk asks, which one would you like? Then, you say, I'll take the blue one, please. Finally, the clerk wraps your present in wrapping paper and you pay.
